美好生活

59| 162|教育留學 60| 163|退休生活 61| 164|身分規劃 62| 165|

67| 170|
L

Ding Yao Lin - Founder, DingYao Advisory

Date: 2026-05-13 · Reading time: 8 minutes

Founder of DingYao Advisory, specializing in offshore asset allocation for Asia-Pacific high-net-worth investors, with deep expertise in Cape Town property market, helping Taiwanese investors achieve stable and tax-efficient offshore rental income.

1|# Foreign Capital Sells TSMC for Three Days: Cape Town Property Becomes the Safe Haven Amid Taiwan Stock Volatility

2|

3|## Introduction

4|

5|When foreign investors sell TSMC for three consecutive days—accumulating nearly 28 million shares sold—Taiwanese investors must ask: where should capital flow? The VIX index has climbed to 17.99, market volatility is intensifying, and the single-day VaR (Value at Risk) reaches -2.89%, meaning that with 95% confidence, the maximum single-day loss could approach 3%. In this turbulent environment, more high-net-worth investors are reconsidering their asset allocation, turning their gaze to Cape Town, South Africa—a real estate market offering stable 8-10% rental yields with annual volatility of only about 5%.

6|

7|---

8|

9|## I. Foreign Capital Exit from TSMC: Data Analysis

10|

11|### 1.1 Foreign Investor Movement Analysis

12|

13|According to the May 13, 2026 Venus Team risk assessment report, foreign investors have sold TSMC (2330) for 3 consecutive days, with cumulative sales volume reaching 27,848,643 shares. Behind this wave of selling may be multiple factors:

14|

15|- Global capital rotation: US dollar index trends and US Treasury yield changes affecting emerging market capital flows

16|- Geopolitical risk: Cross-strait tensions continue to draw international attention, prompting risk-averse capital to exit

17|- Profit-taking: TSMC's stock price has seen significant gains over the past year; foreign investors are taking profits

18|

19|Notably, domestic investment trusts have switched to buying, with a single-day purchase of 8,879,689 shares, indicating domestic capital still has confidence in TSMC. However, proprietary traders have sold for 6 consecutive days, with cumulative sales of 1,468,406 shares, reflecting short-term capital caution.

20|

21|### 1.2 Volatility and Risk Indicators

22|

23|| Indicator | Value | Interpretation |

24||-----------|-------|----------------|

25|| VIX Index | 17.99 | Approaching 18, above historical average, increased market volatility |

26|| VaR 95% | -2.89% | Maximum single-day loss with 95% confidence |

27|| VaR 99% | -4.23% | Maximum single-day loss with 99% confidence |

28|| Maximum Drawdown | 12.37% | Maximum loss during the period |

29|| Volatility | 38.31% | Annualized volatility, relatively high |

30|| Beta | -0.09 | Negative correlation with the broader market |

31|

32|What does 38.31% volatility mean? With TSMC's closing price at NT$2,255, one standard deviation price movement range is approximately ±NT$864. In contrast, Cape Town's real estate market has annual volatility of only about 5%, with stable and predictable price trends.

33|

34|---

35|

36|## II. Taiwanese Investors' Dilemma: High Risk vs. Low Return

37|

38|### 2.1 Risk Concentration in Stock Investment

39|

40|Taiwanese investors commonly face the problem of "heavy concentration in a single market." Take a typical investment portfolio:

41|

42|| Asset Type | Allocation | Risk Characteristics |

43||------------|------------|---------------------|

44|| Taiwan stocks (semiconductors) | 60-70% | High volatility, industry concentration |

45|| Taiwan real estate | 20-30% | Volume contraction, declining prices, low liquidity |

46|| Cash deposits | 5-10% | Low interest rate at 1.5% |

47|

48|The problem with this allocation: when Taiwan stocks fluctuate and Taiwan's real estate market is sluggish, the portfolio lacks hedging instruments. The risk score reaches 6/10 (medium-high risk), and systematic risk in a single market cannot be diversified.

49|

50|### 2.2 Limitations of High-Dividend ETFs

51|

52|Many investors turn to high-dividend ETFs (such as 00878 Cathay永續高股息, 00919 群益台灣精選高息), pursuing 4-5% annual dividend yield. However:

53|

54|- Price volatility risk: ETF NAV fluctuates with the broader market; dividend income may be offset by price losses

55|- Tax costs: Dividend income over NT$20,000 is subject to 28% separate taxation

56|- Industry concentration: Taiwan stock ETFs are heavily concentrated in electronics, lacking industry diversification

57|

58|For example, 00878 closed at NT$28.13 on May 13, 2026, with a single-day decline of 0.53%. Although long-term holding can earn dividends, short-term price volatility may still cause paper losses.

59|

60|---

61|

62|## III. Cape Town Property: A Stable Choice in the Era of Taiwan Stock Volatility

63|

64|### 3.1 Why Cape Town?

65|

66|When foreign capital exits TSMC and Taiwan stocks fluctuate intensively, Cape Town property offers a completely different investment logic:

67|

68|| Comparison | Taiwan Stock Investment | Cape Town Property |

69||------------|------------------------|---------------------|

70|| Annual Volatility | 38.31% | ~5% |

71|| Expected Return | 4-5% (dividend yield) | 8-10% (effective yield) |

72|| Tax Cost | 28% separate taxation | Rental income tax 18-45% (deductible) |

73|| Management Effort | Daily monitoring | Full-service rental management |

74|| Currency Factor | Stable (NTD) | Entry advantage (ZAR depreciation) |

75|

76|### 3.2 Cape Town Rental Yield Analysis

77|

78|According to DingYao Advisory's Cape Town team verified data:

79|

80|| Area | Gross Yield | Effective Yield (Managed) | Notes |

81||------|-------------|--------------------------|--------|

82|| Atlantic Seaboard | 3-4% | 6-8% | Sea view luxury, high appreciation potential |

83|| City Bowl | 4-5% | 7-9% | City center, strong rental demand |

84|| Southern Suburbs | 5-6% | 8-10% | Family market, accessible prices |

85|| Garden Route | 5-7% | 8-11% | Tourism-driven, seasonal variation |

86|

87|Key Distinction: Gross Yield vs. Effective Yield

88|

89|- Gross Yield: Rental income ÷ property price (before expenses)

90|- Effective Yield: Actual cash flow ÷ property price (after management, maintenance, vacancy)

91|

92|Atlantic Seaboard luxury properties (R10M+ / NT$20M+) have gross yields of only 3-4%, but through professional rental management packages, effective yields can reach 6-8%. Reasons:

93|

94|1. Tourism premium: Peak season (December-February) can fetch 2-3x monthly rent

95|2. Professional management: Average occupancy >95%, far above market average of 85%

96|3. Zero vacancy gaps: Rental management package guarantees fixed monthly income

97|

98|### 3.3 Entry Timing: ZAR Depreciation Advantage

99|

100|2026 exchange rate reference: 1 ZAR (South African Rand) ≈ NT$2.

101|

102|ZAR is relatively weak compared to USD and EUR, meaning Taiwanese investors can enter at more favorable prices. Taking a R5M property as example:

103|

104|- Property price: R5,000,000 ≈ NT$10,000,000

105|- Monthly rent: R25,000 ≈ NT$50,000

106|- Annual rental income: NT$600,000

107|- Effective yield: 6% (through rental management)

108|

109|If ZAR appreciates 10% in the future, investors gain currency profits in addition to rental income.

110|

111|---

112|

113|## IV. Risk Assessment: Cape Town vs. Taiwan Stocks

114|

115|### 4.1 Risk Matrix Comparison

116|

117|| Risk Type | Taiwan Stock Investment | Cape Town Property |

118||-----------|-------------------------|---------------------|

119|| Market Volatility | High (38%) | Low (~5%) |

120|| Liquidity Risk | Low (T+2) | Medium (requires 3-5 year holding) |

121|| Currency Risk | None | Yes (ZAR fluctuation) |

122|| Policy Risk | Medium (geopolitics) | Low (sound legal system) |

123|| Management Risk | Low | Low (rental management) |

124|

125|Notably, Cape Town property's currency risk during the current ZAR weakness period is actually an entry advantage. Meanwhile, Taiwan stock geopolitical risk is difficult to eliminate.

126|

127|### 4.2 Asset Allocation Recommendation

128|

129|Recommended Allocation (using NT$10 million as example):

130|

131|| Asset Type | Allocation | Amount | Purpose |

132||------------|-----------|--------|---------|

133|| Taiwan stocks / ETFs | 40% | NT$4 million | Growth assets |

134|| Cape Town property | 30% | NT$3 million | Stable cash flow |

135|| Taiwan deposits / bonds | 20% | NT$2 million | Liquidity reserve |

136|| Gold / Others | 10% | NT$1 million | Hedging allocation |

137|

138|Cape Town property's role is to provide stable cash flow, complementing Taiwan stocks' high volatility. When Taiwan stocks fall, Cape Town rental income continues to come in, reducing overall portfolio volatility.

139|

140|---

141|

142|## V. Practical Operations: How to Invest in Cape Town Property?

143|

144|### 5.1 Entry Thresholds

145|

146|| Entry Price | Example Properties | Target Yield |

147||--------------|-------------------|--------------|

148|| NT$9 million (~R4.5M) | City Bowl 2BR, Southern Suburbs 3BR | 8-10% effective yield |

149|| NT$15 million (~R7.5M) | Atlantic Seaboard 2BR, City Bowl luxury | 6-8% effective yield |

150|| NT$20M+ (~R10M+) | Atlantic Seaboard sea view | 3-4% gross → 6-8% effective yield |

151|

152|### 5.2 DingYao Advisory Full-Service Package

153|

154|Pain points of Cape Town property investment: language barriers, unfamiliar regulations, difficult rental management. DingYao Advisory offers complete solutions:

155|

156|1. Property Selection: Recommend suitable areas and properties based on budget and target yield

157|2. Legal Consultation: South African licensed lawyers assist with title transfer, contract review

158|3. Bank Account Opening: Assist with opening South African bank accounts for rental remittance

159|4. Rental Management: Professional team handles leasing, collection, maintenance; guaranteed fixed monthly income

160|5. Tax Filing: South African tax consultants assist with annual declarations

161|

162|---

163|

164|## VI. Conclusion and Action Recommendations

165|

166|### 6.1 Core Conclusions

167|

168|Foreign investors selling TSMC for three consecutive days reminds us: single-market risk cannot be ignored. When VIX reaches 18 and volatility hits 38%, investors need to consider portfolio diversification.

169|

170|Cape Town property offers a unique solution:

171|

172|- Yield Advantage: 8-10% effective yield, higher than Taiwan stock ETFs' 4-5%

173|- Volatility Advantage: ~5% annual volatility, far lower than Taiwan stocks' 38%

174|- Timing Advantage: ZAR depreciation, favorable entry prices

175|- Management Advantage: Rental management services, no need to handle rental matters personally

176|

177|### 6.2 Action Recommendations

178|

179|| Investor Type | Recommended Action |

180||---------------|-------------------|

181|| Holding Taiwan stocks | Consider allocating some profit-taking funds to Cape Town property for risk diversification |

182|| Idle capital | Good entry timing: weak ZAR + mature rental management |

183|| Retirement planning | Cape Town property provides stable cash flow, suitable for retirement planning |

184|

185|---

186|

187|## FAQ

188|

189|Q: How long does it take for Cape Town rental income to be remitted back to Taiwan?

190|A: Through a South African bank account, rental income can be remitted monthly. South Africa's exchange control allows overseas remittances; DingYao team assists with account opening and remittance processes.

191|

192|Q: Can foreigners buy property in South Africa?

193|A: Yes. South Africa allows foreigners to purchase real estate with freehold title that is permanently valid and inheritable. Foreigner loan limit is 50% of property price; cash purchase is recommended to avoid loan procedures.

194|

195|Q: How is Cape Town property liquidity?

196|A: Cape Town property is a long-term investment, recommended holding period of 3-5 years or more. If liquidity is needed, properties can be sold through local real estate agents, with average selling cycle of about 6-12 months.

197|

198|Q: How to ensure reliability of rental management?

199|A: Choose reputable management companies (such as DingYao partner teams), sign formal rental management contracts, receive monthly financial reports, and have rental income directly deposited into designated accounts.

200|

201|---

202|

210|

217|

Please consult professional advisors before investing. Cape Town rental yields are estimates; actual returns are subject to contract terms.
